categories.name: common
categories.description: Common drugs are those which are well known and widely used among the drug community. This doesn't necessarily mean they are safe, but it usually comes with a longer relative history of use in humans with which to establish a safety profile.

categories.name: psychedelic
categories.description: Psychedelics are drugs which alter the perception, causing a number of mental effects which manifest in many forms including altered states of consciousness, visual or tactile effects.

categories.name: stimulant
categories.description: Stimulants excite the nervous system and increase physiological function.

categories.name: entactogen
categories.description: Entactogens (also known as empathogens) are a class of psychoactive substances that produce distinctive emotional and social effects similar to those of MDMA.

categories.name: depressant
categories.description: Depressants are drugs which reduce arousal and stimulation in the user, characterised by a depressing of mental and physical functions.

categories.name: opioid
categories.description: Opioids are pain-killing depressants which may also cause euphoria.

categories.name: habit-forming
categories.description: These drugs pose a higher risk of causing habit forming behaviour, take particular care with the amount and frequency they are taken.

categories.name: research-chemical
categories.description: Research chemicals are drugs with relatively little history of human use, and thus particular care should be taken if choosing to ingest them.

categories.name: tentative
categories.description: Tentative means that there is not much reliable information about it. This is often because the drug is very new. Information listed under these drugs should not be entirely trusted.

categories.name: dissociative
categories.description: Dissociatives are mostly NMDA receptor antagonists, these substances are hallucinogenic but different than psychedelics. As per the name, these substances create a distance between the user and reality.

categories.name: benzodiazepine
categories.description: Benzodiazepines are generally hypnotic or anxiolytic depressant drugs.

categories.name: cannabinoid
categories.description: A cannabinoid is one of a class of diverse chemical compounds that act on cannabinoid receptors on cells that alter neurotransmitter functioning in the brain.

categories.name: nootropic
categories.description: Nootropics (also referred to as smart drugs, neuro enhancers, and cognitive enhancers) are substances that purportedly improve cognitive functions such as memory, motivation, attention, and concentration, in healthy individuals.

categories.name: deliriant
categories.description: Deliriants are considered to be true hallucinogens, because the visuals they produce are hard or impossible to tell apart from reality; they are also known for negative physical effects.

categories.name: barbiturate
categories.description: Barbiturates (also known as barbs and barbies) are a class of psychoactive substances that act as depressants of the central nervous system. They produce a variety of sedative-hypnotic effects including sedation, anxiety suppression, muscle relaxation, and seizure suppression when administered.

categories.name: eugeroic
categories.description: Eugeroics, also known as wakefulness-promoting agents and wakefulness-promoting drugs, are a class of psychoactive substances that promote wakefulness and alertness. The best-known member of this class is modafinil.

categories.name: hallucinogen
categories.description: Hallucinogens are a class of psychoactive substances that produce powerful alterations in perception, mood, and various cognitive processes. Hallucinogens can be classified into psychedelics, dissociatives and deliriants

categories.name: oneirogen
categories.description: Oneirogen describes a wide array of psychoactive plants and chemicals ranging from normal dream enhancers to intense dissociative or deliriant drugs.

categories.name: antipsychotic
categories.description: Antipsychotics (also known as neuroleptics or major tranquilizers) are a class of psychiatric medication primarily used to manage psychosis (including delusions, hallucinations, or disordered thought), particularly in schizophrenia and bipolar disorder.

categories.name: antidepressant
categories.description: Antidepressants are a class of psychoactive substances that are prescribed to treat psychiatric disorders, most commonly major depressive disorder and some forms of anxiety disorders.

categories.name: hypnotic
categories.description: Hypnotics, or soporifics, are a class of drugs which promote and induce sleep. They are the opposite of eugeroics which promote wakefulness. They are commonly distributed in the form of pharmaceutical drugs prescribed to treat insomnia.

substances.name: Alprazolam

tolerance.full: within a couple of days of continuous use

toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like alcohol, opioids, or barbiturates
substances.summary: An extremely common benzodiazepine better known as Xanax, frequently prescribed for the treatment of anxiety and panic disorders. Alprazolam is short-lasting and primarily anxiolytic, though also possesses hypnotic properties. At high doses amnesia and loss of inhibition are common. Do not mix with other depressants.
substances.effectsSummary:
Anti-excitant and anti-anxiety, sedative, soporific and muscle relaxant. At high doses: slowing down, drowsiness, danger of memory lapses ("film tear").
Taking benzodiazepines increases the effectiveness of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) at the GABA-A receptor. This triggers sedative (calming), hypnotic, anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving), anticonvulsant (anticonvulsant) and muscle-relaxing effects in the body. Benzodiazepines have a depressant effect on the central nervous system. The flow of information in the brain between the brain cells (neurons) is thereby reduced / disturbed and feelings and perceptions are dampened.
The breakdown of the individual active ingredients of benzodiazepines in the body is age-dependent and therefore varies from person to person.
substances.generalRisks:
There is little information available on risks, toxicity, side effects and long-term consequences. Therefore, the general information on benzodiazepines applies:
When mixing with other downers (alcohol, GHB/GBL, heroin) there is a risk of respiratory paralysis!
Taking benzodiazepines can cause numerous undesirable side effects. In addition, regular and long-term use carries a very high risk of physical and psychological dependence. Benzodiazepines should only be taken as prescribed by a doctor and only for a short period of time (max. 4-6 weeks). Longer-term use should be discussed with the treating specialist. The dosages and duration of action of the individual benzodiazepines differ considerably.
Side effects of benzodiazepines may be as follows: Prolonged fatigue, gastrointestinal problems, impaired reactions, hypersensitivity reactions, headaches, dizziness, motor difficulties, visual disturbances, slowed breathing, muscle weakness, confusion, sexual dysfunction, aggression, outbursts of anger, restlessness, random movements, allergies, skin problems/rashes and speech and movement disorders. Some benzodiazepines can cause seizures in epileptics.
substances.longtermRisks:
Regular and long-term use can lead to psychological and physical dependence (very high dependence potential). Immediate discontinuation of the drug after prolonged use can lead to negative withdrawal symptoms (including dizziness, physical weakness, inner restlessness, tremors, sleep disturbances, headaches, sweating, nausea, hallucinations and depression).
In addition, seizures and memory disorders/loss and listlessness (hangover effects) can occur. In case of possible dependence, withdrawal should be discussed with a doctor beforehand and the withdrawal should be medically accompanied. It is extremely important that the dose is reduced gradually.
saferUse: If medicines are obtained on the black market or on the internet and not from a pharmacy/medical facility, the contents are unclear. Have the medicine tested for the exact ingredients in a drug check!
Do not rely on dosage information from colleagues who regularly use benzodiazepines. Due to habituation or dependence, their doses can be much higher and fatal for new users.
Blisters of counterfeits may look identical to the original packaging.

The simultaneous consumption of depressant substances such as alcohol, ketamine, GHB/GBL, nitrous oxide, opioids and/or other benzodiazepines is dangerous as there is an increased risk of vomiting and unconsciousness. The risk of suffocation is high!

indications: Anxiety or insomnia-related indications depending on local labeling
contraindications: See current product-specific label and local approved labeling.
majorWarnings: Sedatives can impair driving and respiration; avoid combining with alcohol, opioids, or other CNS depressants unless specifically supervised.
majorInteractions: Drug-specific CYP, QT, serotonergic, CNS depressant, or other interaction risks require product-specific label review.

substances.name: Amphetamine

tolerance.full: develops with prolonged and repeated use

substances.addictionPotential: has high abuse potential and can cause psychological dependence with chronic use
toxicities: exact toxic dosage is unknown
substances.summary: A very popular CNS stimulant available on prescription and black markets. Recreational in high doses, producing mild euphoria and an abundance of energy. Popular in eastern europe and the US.
substances.effectsSummary: The release of the endogenous neurotransmitters noradrenalin and dopamine produced by the use of amphetamine can cause a feeling of increased performance, an increase in self-esteem, an increase in body temperature, the suppression of tiredness, hunger and thirst, an increased willingness to take risks and a suppressed feeling of pain, and can lead to euphoria as well as an increased urge to talk (babble flash).
substances.dosageRemark: Because of the varying purity (the amphetamine content varies from almost 0 % to almost 100 %!), dosing is difficult and the risk of overdose is high. Doses of more than 25 mg of pure amphetamine increase the negative effects. A dose of 50 mg/night should be the maximum, whether snorted or swallowed.
substances.generalRisks: Tremors, restlessness, nausea, palpitations and cardiac arrhythmias, sleep disturbances, headaches, nervousness, irritability, loss of appetite, aggressive behaviour and, in combination with alcohol, danger of alcohol poisoning. At high doses: hallucinations, circulatory failure, strokes as well as kidney, liver and heart failure and states of deep unconsciousness. An often underestimated danger is the rise in body temperature, which can lead to dehydration of the body. In high doses, acute amphetamine psychosis can occur. Signs of this are hallucinations in the form of colour spots and with features of schizophrenia. The risk of such an acute mental disorder increases with consumption over several days and/or if high doses are taken and food intake and sleep are neglected over a longer period of time. Such an experience usually normalises after sufficient sleep. During and after coming down: extreme need for sleep, exhaustion, strong feeling of hunger,depressive mood, irritability, etc.
substances.longtermRisks: There is a risk of addiction with psychological symptoms, especially if the use takes on a (performance-enhancing) function in everyday life (e.g. at work) or physical/psychological exhaustion is combated with amphetamine. Frequent use of amphetamine not infrequently leads to physical deficiency symptoms (e.g. calcium deficiency, which can lead to bone and tooth problems) and to psychological problems such as dejection or listlessness. Regular and high-dose use can lead to constant restlessness (but also persistent fatigue), sleep and circulatory disorders as well as anxiety disorders up to persistent amphetamine psychosis with paranoid delusions. In addition, high blood pressure, cardiac arrhythmias, weight loss, skin inflammations ("speed pimples"), stomach problems as well as liver and kidney damage and cramps are possible. Snorting damages the nasal mucous membranes and the nasal septum, swallowing damages the stomach mucous membranes. Chronic consumption also favours brain haemorrhages and strokes with sudden paralysis.
saferUse: Dose low. Think about how long you want to be awake before you take it! Do not suppress your need for sleep.
The lowest-risk form of amphetamine use is to swallow it.
Eat enough after consumption to prevent weight loss.
Drink enough (non-alcoholic drinks).
Take extra vitamin C and D and minerals (iron, calcium and magnesium) with frequent use.
Follow the safer-sniffing rules when sniffing.
Refrain from mixed consumption! Make sure to take breaks from consumption.
Mentally ill persons, persons with high blood pressure, liver and kidney diseases, diabetics and pregnant women should refrain from using amphetamines.
It is better not to wear headgear (danger of overheating!).
Amphetamine pastes should always be well dried before consumption.

substances.name: Buprenorphine

tolerance.full: develops with prolonged and repeated use

substances.addictionPotential: moderately addictive with a high potential for abuse
toxicities: low toxicity
substances.summary: A semi-synthetic opioid analgesic also known as subutex. Often administered as Butrans patches, or in combination with Naloxone as Suboxone, a heroin replacement therapy drug. Will cause withdrawals if you have an opioid tolerance and don't wait long enough (typically around 48 hours) before taking this.
substances.effectsSummary: Like all opiates, buprenorphine has analgesic and cough-irritant effects. However, its euphoric and sedative properties are less pronounced than those of other opioids, which is why it is also used for substitution treatment. Buprenorphine is a partial agonist. This means that, for safety reasons, only a submaximal effect is elicited. After a certain dose, a saturation effect (ceiling effect) occurs, which prevents a further increase in the effect. This makes the active ingredient so relevant for substitution treatments, as withdrawal symptoms can be alleviated and overdoses avoided.
substances.dosageRemark:
From 16 mg, the saturation effect (ceiling effect) takes effect and no more effect enhancement is felt.
When consuming for the first time, low doses should be used, as the risk of respiratory arrest is increased.
substances.generalRisks:
The side effects are less pronounced than with other opioids. Nevertheless, fatigue, constipation, drowsiness, sweating, nausea, vomiting, insomnia and irritability may occur. Buprenorphine has a lower risk of respiratory depression, which is why it is considered comparatively safe.
Overdoses with buprenorphine are difficult to treat with the opioid antagonist naloxone due to its high binding to the μ-opioid receptor. Therefore, agents from the group of respiratory stimulants (such as doxapram) are used.
substances.longtermRisks: Unlike other opioids, the active ingredient floods on slowly, which is why the risk of dependence is considered lower.
saferUse: Opioids are highly effective drugs that should only be used for a limited time and at best under medical supervision.
Start with a low dose and wait for the effect and tolerance before adding more. Do not exceed the maximum daily dose.

If you inject opioids, dose even more carefully, as the range between desired effect (rush) and dangerous overdose is even more difficult to assess. Avoid injecting fentanyl; the risk of overdose is particularly high. Always use new (clean and sterile) injection material! Never exchange syringes, filters, water, disinfection swabs to avoid transmission of hepatitis and HIV.
Do not rely on dosage information from colleagues who regularly use opioids. Due to habituation or dependence, their doses are much higher and can be fatal for new users.
Take longer breaks (at least several days) between consumption.
After a period of abstinence, take a much lower dose! The usual dose before the abstinence phase can otherwise quickly have life-threatening consequences.

The simultaneous consumption of depressant substances such as alcohol, ketamine, GHB/GBL, nitrous oxide, benzodiazepines and/or other opioids is dangerous as there is an increased risk of vomiting and unconsciousness. The risk of suffocation is high!
Refrain from citrus fruits (especially grapefruit) before or during consumption. The combination can lead to an increase in the effect of the opiate and/or to respiratory depression.

substances.name: Diazepam

tolerance.full: within a couple of days of continuous use

toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like alcohol or opioids
substances.summary: A very common and widely prescribed benzodiazepine with hypnotic and sedative qualities. The metre by which other benzodiazepines are compared. May cause amnesia and lowered inhibitions in excess. Has a relatively long half-life in comparison with other benzodiazepines.
substances.effectsSummary:
Diazepam has a calming, relaxing, antispasmodic, anti-anxiety and sleep-inducing effect.
Taking benzodiazepines increases the effectiveness of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) at the GABA-A receptor. This triggers sedative (calming), hypnotic, anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving), anticonvulsant (anticonvulsant) and muscle-relaxing effects in the body. Benzodiazepines have a depressant effect on the central nervous system. The flow of information in the brain between the brain cells (neurons) is thereby reduced / disturbed and feelings and perceptions are dampened.
The breakdown of the individual active ingredients of benzodiazepines in the body is age-dependent and therefore varies from person to person.
substances.generalRisks:
There is little information available on risks, toxicity, side effects and long-term consequences. Therefore, the general information on benzodiazepines applies:
When mixing with other downers (alcohol, GHB/GBL, heroin) there is a risk of respiratory paralysis!
Taking benzodiazepines can cause numerous undesirable side effects. In addition, regular and long-term use carries a very high risk of physical and psychological dependence. Benzodiazepines should only be taken as prescribed by a doctor and only for a short period of time (max. 4-6 weeks). Longer-term use should be discussed with the treating specialist. The dosages and duration of action of the individual benzodiazepines differ considerably.
Side effects of benzodiazepines may be as follows: Prolonged fatigue, gastrointestinal problems, impaired reactions, hypersensitivity reactions, headaches, dizziness, motor difficulties, visual disturbances, slowed breathing, muscle weakness, confusion, sexual dysfunction, aggression, outbursts of anger, restlessness, random movements, allergies, skin problems/rashes and speech and movement disorders. Some benzodiazepines can cause seizures in epileptics.
substances.longtermRisks:
Regular and long-term use can lead to psychological and physical dependence (very high dependence potential). Immediate discontinuation of the drug after prolonged use can lead to negative withdrawal symptoms (including dizziness, physical weakness, inner restlessness, tremors, sleep disturbances, headaches, sweating, nausea, hallucinations and depression).
In addition, seizures and memory disorders/loss and listlessness (hangover effects) can occur. In case of possible dependence, withdrawal should be discussed with a doctor beforehand and the withdrawal should be medically accompanied. It is extremely important that the dose is reduced gradually.
saferUse: If medicines are obtained on the black market or on the internet and not from a pharmacy/medical facility, the contents are unclear. Have the medicine tested for the exact ingredients in a drug check!
Do not rely on dosage information from colleagues who regularly use benzodiazepines. Due to habituation or dependence, their doses can be much higher and fatal for new users.
Blisters of counterfeits may look identical to the original packaging.

The simultaneous consumption of depressant substances such as alcohol, ketamine, GHB/GBL, nitrous oxide, opioids and/or other benzodiazepines is dangerous as there is an increased risk of vomiting and unconsciousness. The risk of suffocation is high!

substances.summary: A thienodiazepine anxiolytic. Similar in action to benzodiazepine drugs. Relieves anxiety, causes sedation and mild euphoria. Can be found in pressed pharmaceutical pills from various countries, clandestine pill presses, or as a powder

substances.name: Flunitrazepam

tolerance.full: within a couple of days of continuous use

toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like alcohol or opioids
substances.summary: Known as Rohypnol or 'roofies,' this infamous drug has similar qualities to most other benzodiazepines; sedating with strong hypnotic effects. Despite being known as a 'date rape drug' has only been implicated in a small number of such crimes. Danger of respiratory depression in combination with other depressants. May cause amnesia and lowered inhibitions in overdose.
substances.effectsSummary:
Flunitrazepam has a calming, relaxing, antispasmodic, anti-anxiety and sleep-inducing effect.
Taking benzodiazepines increases the effectiveness of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) at the GABA-A receptor. This triggers sedative (calming), hypnotic, anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving), anticonvulsant (anticonvulsant) and muscle-relaxing effects in the body. Benzodiazepines have a depressant effect on the central nervous system. The flow of information in the brain between the brain cells (neurons) is thereby reduced / disturbed and feelings and perceptions are dampened.
The breakdown of the individual active ingredients of benzodiazepines in the body is age-dependent and therefore varies from person to person.
substances.generalRisks:
There is little information available on risks, toxicity, side effects and long-term consequences. Therefore, the general information on benzodiazepines applies:
When mixing with other downers (alcohol, GHB/GBL, heroin) there is a risk of respiratory paralysis!
Taking benzodiazepines can cause numerous undesirable side effects. In addition, regular and long-term use carries a very high risk of physical and psychological dependence. Benzodiazepines should only be taken as prescribed by a doctor and only for a short period of time (max. 4-6 weeks). Longer-term use should be discussed with the treating specialist. The dosages and duration of action of the individual benzodiazepines differ considerably.
Side effects of benzodiazepines may be as follows: Prolonged fatigue, gastrointestinal problems, impaired reactions, hypersensitivity reactions, headaches, dizziness, motor difficulties, visual disturbances, slowed breathing, muscle weakness, confusion, sexual dysfunction, aggression, outbursts of anger, restlessness, random movements, allergies, skin problems/rashes and speech and movement disorders. Some benzodiazepines can cause seizures in epileptics.
substances.longtermRisks:
Regular and long-term use can lead to psychological and physical dependence (very high dependence potential). Immediate discontinuation of the drug after prolonged use can lead to negative withdrawal symptoms (including dizziness, physical weakness, inner restlessness, tremors, sleep disturbances, headaches, sweating, nausea, hallucinations and depression).
In addition, seizures and memory disorders/loss and listlessness (hangover effects) can occur. In case of possible dependence, withdrawal should be discussed with a doctor beforehand and the withdrawal should be medically accompanied. It is extremely important that the dose is reduced gradually.
saferUse: If medicines are obtained on the black market or on the internet and not from a pharmacy/medical facility, the contents are unclear. Have the medicine tested for the exact ingredients in a drug check!
Do not rely on dosage information from colleagues who regularly use benzodiazepines. Due to habituation or dependence, their doses can be much higher and fatal for new users.
Blisters of counterfeits may look identical to the original packaging.

The simultaneous consumption of depressant substances such as alcohol, ketamine, GHB/GBL, nitrous oxide, opioids and/or other benzodiazepines is dangerous as there is an increased risk of vomiting and unconsciousness. The risk of suffocation is high!

substances.name: Ketamine

tolerance.full: develops with prolonged and repeated use

substances.addictionPotential: moderate to high abuse potential and produces psychological dependence with chronic use
substances.summary: A short acting dissociative anaesthetic and hallucinogen commonly used in emergency medicine. It is the prototypical dissociative, and is widely used at sub-anesthetic doses recreationally. Small doses are comparable with alcohol, while larger doses are immobilising and lead to psychedelic experiences: the "K-Hole."
substances.effectsSummary:
The effect of ketamine as an intoxicant is strongly dependent on the dosage: In lower doses, it has a disinhibiting and relaxing effect similar to alcohol, while higher doses can induce trance-like states up to out-of-body or near-death experiences (K-Hole). There can be a fragmentary dissolution of the environment and bodily sensation, thoughts can break off, feelings of weightlessness or floating can appear. Sensory perceptions and the sense of space-time change. At higher doses, detachment from one's own body and/or ego dissolution or merging with the environment may occur.
After the trip: drowsiness, memory of the experience is often only partially possible.
substances.dosageRemark: The dosage of ketamine depends strongly on the desired effect.
substances.generalRisks:
Movement and communication may be severely restricted. Partial or complete insensitivity to pain, coordination disorders, sensation of weakness, loss of appetite, nausea, vomiting, uncoordinated muscle movements, dizziness, slurred speech, increased pulse and blood pressure and cardiac arrhythmias. At high doses, muscle stiffness, paralysis and narcosis, at very high doses, epileptic seizures and coma.
Ketamine puts a strain on the cardiovascular system. A ketamine trip can be psychologically very stressful. Many ketamine users report near-death experiences, nightmare hallucinations, tunnel visions, blackouts and short periods of memory loss.
After repeated consumption within a short period of time, the effect diminishes considerably and a tolerance develops.
Ketamine taken intravenously quicker than 1.5 minute can cause breathing depression for up to a minute.
substances.longtermRisks: Ketamine can cause dependence with psychological symptoms. Chronic use damages the liver and kidneys and can lead to depressive moods and anxiety. It is suspected that even in small doses, ketamine can trigger dysfunctions in areas of the brain responsible for memory, learning and perception. The more often it is consumed and the larger the individual doses, the more alarming these disturbances become.
saferUse: Ketamine is not a party drug! Do not take ketamine alone and make sure you are in a familiar and comfortable environment. Allow enough time to process the trip in peace afterwards.
Dose carefully. Small differences in dose can cause significant differences in effect, so dosing is difficult.
Take regular breaks from consumption.
You need a place to sit or lie down, because at high doses there is a great risk of collapsing or fainting.
Because of the insensitivity to pain, you can hurt yourself without noticing it.
Do not take ketamine on a full stomach.

Refrain from mixed consumption. When combined with alcohol, benzodiazepines or opiates, there is a risk of respiratory arrest! Mixing with uppers (e.g. cocaine) can lead to circulatory problems, increase in heart rate and shortness of breath.
People with cardiovascular problems, high blood pressure or glaucoma should absolutely refrain from consuming ketamine!

substances.name: Melatonin

tolerance.full: after prolonged and repeated usage

toxicities: extremely low toxicity
substances.summary: A naturally occurring hormone produced in the body, which promotes sleep at certain times in the day based on the circadian rhythm. It is also commonly available as a drug to treat insomnia and promote a proper sleep cycle. It can be used to promote sleepfulness at the tail-end of drug experiences, though it is not particularly hypnotic.

substances.name: Methylphenidate
substances.addictionPotential: moderately addictive with a high potential for abuse
substances.summary: A psychostimulant commonly used in the treatment of ADHD, narcolepsy and obesity, particularly in the EU instead of Adderall. Methylphenidate is also a 5HT1A receptor agonist. Sometimes prescribed off-label to help the withdrawals from cocaine and other stimulants.
substances.effectsSummary:
Taking methylphenidate increases the concentration of the nerve messengers dopamine, noradrenaline and serotonin in the brain. When prescribed for ADHD, the drug is supposed to act as a filter for incoming stimuli and alleviate symptoms such as distractibility, attention deficit disorder, disorganisation and impulsivity.
However, due to its stimulating, energising and drive-enhancing properties, methylphenidate is also used as a party drug or to increase performance. Due to the increased release of dopamine, similar effects occur as with the use of cocaine.
substances.generalRisks:
If prescribed by a doctor or used as directed, sleep disturbances and irritability can often occur. Eating disorders, stomach complaints, nausea and vomiting are also known side effects.
Abusive use and higher doses can lead to seizures, cardiac arrhythmias as well as headaches and confusion.
Tolerance develops relatively quickly, which is why higher and higher doses have to be consumed. In overdose, Ritalin can trigger hallucinations.
substances.longtermRisks: It is assumed that no dependence develops with therapeutic use of Ritalin. When abusing higher doses over a longer period of time, psychological dependence may develop. With abrupt discontinuation, withdrawal symptoms such as lethargy, apathy, depression and paranoia may occur.
saferUse: If possible, do not consume methylphenidate intravenously. Due to the substances contained in the tablets, embolism (blockage of lung or brain vessels) can occur.

indications: Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder depending on local labeling
contraindications: See current product-specific label and local approved labeling.
majorWarnings: Cardiovascular, psychiatric activation, sleep, appetite, and misuse risks are formulation- and patient-specific.
majorInteractions: Drug-specific CYP, QT, serotonergic, CNS depressant, or other interaction risks require product-specific label review.

substances.name: Midazolam
substances.summary: A common hypnotic, sedative and anxiolytic benzodiazepine. High doses may cause amnesia and loss of inhibitions. Unusually, it is water soluble, and commonly used as a premedication for sedation as the solubility makes it better for IV use than other benzodiazepines.
substances.effectsSummary:
Midazolam has a calming, relaxing, antispasmodic, anti-anxiety and sleep-inducing effect.
Taking benzodiazepines increases the effectiveness of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) at the GABA-A receptor. This triggers sedative (calming), hypnotic, anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving), anticonvulsant (anticonvulsant) and muscle-relaxing effects in the body. Benzodiazepines have a depressant effect on the central nervous system. The flow of information in the brain between the brain cells (neurons) is thereby reduced / disturbed and feelings and perceptions are dampened.
The breakdown of the individual active ingredients of benzodiazepines in the body is age-dependent and therefore varies from person to person.
substances.generalRisks:
There is little information available on risks, toxicity, side effects and long-term consequences. Therefore, the general information on benzodiazepines applies:
When mixing with other downers (alcohol, GHB/GBL, heroin) there is a risk of respiratory paralysis!
Taking benzodiazepines can cause numerous undesirable side effects. In addition, regular and long-term use carries a very high risk of physical and psychological dependence. Benzodiazepines should only be taken as prescribed by a doctor and only for a short period of time (max. 4-6 weeks). Longer-term use should be discussed with the treating specialist. The dosages and duration of action of the individual benzodiazepines differ considerably.
Side effects of benzodiazepines may be as follows: Prolonged fatigue, gastrointestinal problems, impaired reactions, hypersensitivity reactions, headaches, dizziness, motor difficulties, visual disturbances, slowed breathing, muscle weakness, confusion, sexual dysfunction, aggression, outbursts of anger, restlessness, random movements, allergies, skin problems/rashes and speech and movement disorders. Some benzodiazepines can cause seizures in epileptics.
substances.longtermRisks:
Regular and long-term use can lead to psychological and physical dependence (very high dependence potential). Immediate discontinuation of the drug after prolonged use can lead to negative withdrawal symptoms (including dizziness, physical weakness, inner restlessness, tremors, sleep disturbances, headaches, sweating, nausea, hallucinations and depression).
In addition, seizures and memory disorders/loss and listlessness (hangover effects) can occur. In case of possible dependence, withdrawal should be discussed with a doctor beforehand and the withdrawal should be medically accompanied. It is extremely important that the dose is reduced gradually.
saferUse: If medicines are obtained on the black market or on the internet and not from a pharmacy/medical facility, the contents are unclear. Have the medicine tested for the exact ingredients in a drug check!
Do not rely on dosage information from colleagues who regularly use benzodiazepines. Due to habituation or dependence, their doses can be much higher and fatal for new users.
Blisters of counterfeits may look identical to the original packaging.

The simultaneous consumption of depressant substances such as alcohol, ketamine, GHB/GBL, nitrous oxide, opioids and/or other benzodiazepines is dangerous as there is an increased risk of vomiting and unconsciousness. The risk of suffocation is high!

substances.name: Modafinil

tolerance.full: develops with prolonged and repeated use

substances.addictionPotential: not addictive with a low potential for abuse
toxicities: The median lethal dose at which 50% of participants die (LD50) from modafinil for human beings has never been reached
substances.summary: A wakefulness promoting, long acting stimulant which is typically only used functionally, because there is no real euphoric component to its effects. Also prescribed for daytime sleeping disorders.
substances.effectsSummary:
The mechanism of action of modafinil is not yet fully understood. It is assumed that modafinil binds to the dopamine transporter and inhibits dopamine reuptake. Unlike other stimulants such as amphetamine or methylphenidate (Ritalin®, Concerta®), which in equivalent wakefulness-promoting doses increase neuronal activity throughout the brain, modafinil acts primarily on the brain regions responsible for controlling waking, sleep, wakefulness and vigilance (sustained attention).
Modafinil thus promotes alertness (physical and mental), increases attention and motor activity. Due to its stimulating, wakefulness-promoting properties, modafinil is consumed by healthy people as a so-called "smart drug" or "cognitive enhancer".According to user reports, modafinil has a much more subtle effect than other stimulants and does not trigger comparable states of intoxication. Modafinil causes less physical restlessness and the sleep-wake rhythm is less disturbed.
A mood-lifting and euphoric effect is controversial. According to a publication of the American Food and Drug Administration (FDA), modafinil causes psychoactive and euphoric effects, whereas various studies point to the lack of a euphoric effect.
substances.dosageRemark:
The recommended daily dose when prescribed by a doctor is 200 mg at the beginning. If there is an insufficient response to this dose, a maximum daily dose of 400 mg may be administered/taken.
The long-term effect of modafinil when taken regularly and over a longer period of time has not yet been studied. If taken over a longer period of time, regular monitoring by a medical specialist is therefore recommended.
substances.generalRisks:
In 2011, the European Medicines Agency (EMA) recommended limiting the marketing authorisation for modafinil to the treatment of adults with excessive sleepiness associated with narcolepsy. Due to the possible risks and side effects, modafinil has since not been approved for other indications, for the treatment of children and adolescents, and during pregnancy and breastfeeding.
The following side effects may occur: Headache, nervousness, palpitations, abdominal pain and/or dry mouth. In addition, modafinil can impair the effectiveness of oral contraceptives! Therefore, the use of additional contraceptive methods is recommended during intake. Further risks are cardiovascular diseases such as cardiac arrhythmias, diseases of the heart muscles, cardiac insufficiency and/or high blood pressure. Furthermore, the consumption of modafinil can have an effect on the psyche and trigger hostility, aggression ,psychoses/psychotic disorders, depression, insomnia, up to delusions and/or anxiety.
Symptoms of overdose with modafinil alone or in combination with other medicines: Insomnia; central nervous symptoms (restlessness, disorientation, confusion, agitation, hallucinations); nausea, diarrhoea; palpitations, high blood pressure, chest pain, slow heartbeat.
substances.longtermRisks:
Since modafinil affects the dopamine balance in the brain, among other things, there is an increased potential for physical dependence. If modafinil is consumed for the purpose of performance enhancement and promotion of alertness with the intention of being able to achieve high performance with little sleep, there is also a risk of psychological dependence.
The long-term effect of modafinil when taken regularly and over a longer period of time has been little studied so far. If taken over a longer period of time, regular monitoring by a medical specialist is therefore recommended.
saferUse: Caution: The effectiveness of hormonal contraceptives may be impaired by the simultaneous intake of modafinil.
Side effects such as blurred vision and dizziness may impair the ability to drive or operate machinery.
Taking modafinil is not a substitute for sleep! Be sure to maintain good sleep hygiene.

If you are using modafinil for cognitive enhancement, try to think of alternative strategies to improve your memory and recall. Sleep deprivation after taking modafinil in particular can impair cognitive performance.
Take longer breaks (at least several days) between consumption.
The effect of modafinil can last up to 10 hours. Therefore, pay attention to the time of intake.
Remember to drink enough water. This can be forgotten during the effect of modafinil.

substances.name: Oxazepam
toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like alcohol or opioids
substances.summary: A prescription benzodiazepine with intermediate duration and half life. A sedative and hypnotic which may cause lowered inhibitions and amnesia in higher doses.
substances.effectsSummary:
Oxazepam has a calming, relaxing, antispasmodic, anti-anxiety and sleep-inducing effect.
Taking benzodiazepines increases the effectiveness of the neurotransmitter gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) at the GABA-A receptor. This triggers sedative (calming), hypnotic, anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving), anticonvulsant (anticonvulsant) and muscle-relaxing effects in the body. Benzodiazepines have a depressant effect on the central nervous system. The flow of information in the brain between the brain cells (neurons) is thereby reduced / disturbed and feelings and perceptions are dampened.
The breakdown of the individual active ingredients of benzodiazepines in the body is age-dependent and therefore varies from person to person.
substances.generalRisks:
There is little information available on risks, toxicity, side effects and long-term consequences. Therefore, the general information on benzodiazepines applies:
When mixing with other downers (alcohol, GHB/GBL, heroin) there is a risk of respiratory paralysis!
Taking benzodiazepines can cause numerous undesirable side effects. In addition, regular and long-term use carries a very high risk of physical and psychological dependence. Benzodiazepines should only be taken as prescribed by a doctor and only for a short period of time (max. 4-6 weeks). Longer-term use should be discussed with the treating specialist. The dosages and duration of action of the individual benzodiazepines differ considerably.
Side effects of benzodiazepines may be as follows: Prolonged fatigue, gastrointestinal problems, impaired reactions, hypersensitivity reactions, headaches, dizziness, motor difficulties, visual disturbances, slowed breathing, muscle weakness, confusion, sexual dysfunction, aggression, outbursts of anger, restlessness, random movements, allergies, skin problems/rashes and speech and movement disorders. Some benzodiazepines can cause seizures in epileptics.
substances.longtermRisks:
Regular and long-term use can lead to psychological and physical dependence (very high dependence potential). Immediate discontinuation of the drug after prolonged use can lead to negative withdrawal symptoms (including dizziness, physical weakness, inner restlessness, tremors, sleep disturbances, headaches, sweating, nausea, hallucinations and depression).
In addition, seizures and memory disorders/loss and listlessness (hangover effects) can occur. In case of possible dependence, withdrawal should be discussed with a doctor beforehand and the withdrawal should be medically accompanied. It is extremely important that the dose is reduced gradually.
saferUse: If medicines are obtained on the black market or on the internet and not from a pharmacy/medical facility, the contents are unclear. Have the medicine tested for the exact ingredients in a drug check!
Do not rely on dosage information from colleagues who regularly use benzodiazepines. Due to habituation or dependence, their doses can be much higher and fatal for new users.
Blisters of counterfeits may look identical to the original packaging.

The simultaneous consumption of depressant substances such as alcohol, ketamine, GHB/GBL, nitrous oxide, opioids and/or other benzodiazepines is dangerous as there is an increased risk of vomiting and unconsciousness. The risk of suffocation is high!

majorWarnings: Sedatives can impair driving and respiration; avoid combining with alcohol, opioids, or other CNS depressants unless specifically supervised.

substances.name: Triazolam

tolerance.full: within a couple of days of continuous use

toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like alcohol or opioids
substances.summary: An uncommon and very short acting benzodiazepine. Sedating and hypnotic, and may induce amnesia and lowered inhibitions at high doses.

indications: Anxiety or insomnia-related indications depending on local labeling
contraindications: See current product-specific label and local approved labeling.
majorWarnings: Sedatives can impair driving and respiration; avoid combining with alcohol, opioids, or other CNS depressants unless specifically supervised.
majorInteractions: Drug-specific CYP, QT, serotonergic, CNS depressant, or other interaction risks require product-specific label review.

substances.name: Zolpidem
toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like alcohol or opioids
substances.summary: Commonly prescribed for insomnia, Ambien (zolpidem) can cause realistic hallucinations similar to those of deliriants, and is very likely to cause amnesia at higher doses. Take care when using this drug, as it tends to lower inhibitions to a level which causes the user to do things they might not normally do when sober.

indications: Anxiety or insomnia-related indications depending on local labeling
contraindications: See current product-specific label and local approved labeling.
majorWarnings: Sedatives can impair driving and respiration; avoid combining with alcohol, opioids, or other CNS depressants unless specifically supervised.

substances.name: Zopiclone

tolerance.full: within a couple of weeks of daily use

toxicities: low toxicity
potentially lethal when mixed with depressants like benzodiazepines, alcohol or opioids
substances.summary: A nonbenzodiazepine sedative sleep aid (Z-drug), which can have hallucinogenic effects if taken while awake. Often causes users to have a metallic taste in the mouth for ~12h. Has been reported in some cases to cause certain users to black out, redose excessively and undertake dangerous activities such as driving.

indications: Anxiety or insomnia-related indications depending on local labeling
contraindications: See current product-specific label and local approved labeling.
majorWarnings: Sedatives can impair driving and respiration; avoid combining with alcohol, opioids, or other CNS depressants unless specifically supervised.
majorInteractions: Drug-specific CYP, QT, serotonergic, CNS depressant, or other interaction risks require product-specific label review.

substances.name: Lisinopril
commonNames: C09 肾素-血管紧张素系统药物
Agents acting on the renin-angiotensin system
toxicities: ACE inhibitors can cause symptomatic hypotension, renal function deterioration, hyperkalemia, angioedema, cough, and fetal toxicity in pregnancy.
Overdose is expected to mainly cause hypotension; treatment is supportive and clinical.
substances.summary: 赖诺普利是 ACE 抑制剂；资料重点为药代/药效时间进程、监测项目和相互作用索引。 Lisinopril is an ACE inhibitor used in cardiovascular indications. This entry is for clinical pharmacology learning and data indexing only.

drugClass: C09 肾素-血管紧张素系统药物
Agents acting on the renin-angiotensin system
indications: Hypertension
Heart failure
Selected post-myocardial infarction contexts depending on label
contraindications: History of ACE inhibitor-associated angioedema
Concomitant neprilysin inhibitor use or use within 36 hours of sacubitril/valsartan switching
Pregnancy
majorWarnings: Angioedema may occur at any time during ACE inhibitor therapy.
Renal function and potassium require monitoring in susceptible patients.
Hyperkalemia and renal-function worsening can occur.
Pregnancy exposure can cause fetal toxicity; follow the product boxed warning or local labeling.
majorInteractions: Potassium supplements or potassium-sparing diuretics may increase hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs may reduce antihypertensive effect and worsen renal function in susceptible patients.
Lithium toxicity risk may increase.

tmax.note: Peak serum concentrations occur within about 7 hours after oral administration.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Label-derived effective half-life with accumulation; terminal phase reflects ACE binding.

notes: Blood pressure response and adverse effects, not plasma concentration, guide routine clinical use.
Do not use this entry for self-medication or dose adjustment.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical use is usually guided by blood pressure, renal function, potassium, symptoms, and indication-specific follow-up rather than plasma concentration.
interpretationCaveats: Laboratory monitoring here means renal function and electrolytes, not lisinopril plasma TDM.

substances.name: Losartan
commonNames: C09 肾素-血管紧张素系统药物
Agents acting on the renin-angiotensin system
toxicities: ARBs can cause symptomatic hypotension, renal function deterioration, hyperkalemia, and fetal toxicity in pregnancy.
Overdose is expected to mainly cause hypotension and tachycardia or bradycardia; management is supportive.
substances.summary: 氯沙坦是血管紧张素 II 受体阻滞剂（ARB）；资料重点为临床药理学习和数据索引。 Losartan is an angiotensin II receptor blocker. This entry is for clinical pharmacology learning and data indexing only.

drugClass: C09 肾素-血管紧张素系统药物
Agents acting on the renin-angiotensin system
indications: Hypertension
Selected renal and cardiovascular risk-reduction contexts depending on label
contraindications: Pregnancy
Aliskiren coadministration in patients with diabetes
majorWarnings: Renal function and potassium require monitoring in susceptible patients.
Fetal toxicity warning applies to drugs acting on the renin-angiotensin system.
Hyperkalemia and renal-function worsening can occur.
Pregnancy exposure can cause fetal toxicity; follow the product boxed warning or local labeling.
majorInteractions: Potassium supplements or potassium-sparing diuretics may increase hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs may worsen renal function and reduce antihypertensive effect.
Lithium toxicity risk may increase.

tmax.note: Losartan mean peak concentration occurs in about 1 hour; the active metabolite peaks later.

peakEffect.note: Principal active metabolite peak concentration occurs about 3 to 4 hours after dosing.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Losartan half-life is about 2 hours; active metabolite half-life is about 6 to 9 hours.

notes: Tmax and antihypertensive effect timing are not interchangeable.
Use clinical response and safety monitoring rather than plasma concentration for routine care.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Routine monitoring is based on blood pressure, renal function, potassium, symptoms, and indication-specific endpoints rather than plasma concentration.
interpretationCaveats: Losartan has an active metabolite; plasma concentration is not a routine dosing target.

substances.name: Lidocaine
toxicities: Systemic lidocaine toxicity can include neurologic symptoms, seizures, hypotension, conduction abnormalities, arrhythmias, and cardiovascular collapse.
Toxicity risk depends on concentration, infusion timing, hepatic function, acid-base status, and clinical context.
substances.summary: 利多卡因是局部麻醉药和 Ib 类抗心律失常药；本条目区分药代时间进程、TDM 资料和临床处置。 Lidocaine is a local anesthetic and Class Ib antiarrhythmic. This entry separates pharmacokinetic/TDM reference data from clinical treatment decisions and is for educational indexing only.

indications: Ventricular arrhythmias
majorWarnings: High systemic concentrations can be associated with neurologic and cardiovascular toxicity.
majorInteractions: Other sodium channel blockers or antiarrhythmics can have additive cardiac or neurologic toxicity.
Propranolol and other interacting drugs may reduce lidocaine clearance or increase toxicity risk.
monitoring: Serum lidocaine concentration when clinically indicated

tdm.monitoringType: serum or plasma concentration when clinically indicated
tdm.samplingTime: Interpret relative to infusion timing, dose changes, organ function, and symptoms.

therapeuticRanges.note: Laboratory reference interval; not a prescribing target.

toxicityThresholds.note: DailyMed and laboratory references associate increasing adverse manifestations with concentrations above this range.

criticalValues.note: Reported as a critical value by Mayo-style test catalog.

interpretationCaveats: Concentration interpretation must be tied to sampling time and clinical status.
Toxicity assessment also depends on ECG, neurologic symptoms, acid-base status, hepatic function, and interacting drugs.

notes: Antiarrhythmic effect after IV use is rapid; plasma concentration and toxicity interpretation depend on infusion timing and hepatic blood flow.
Source values should be interpreted with ECG, neurologic symptoms, hepatic function, acid-base status, and interacting drugs.

substances.name: Procainamide

majorWarnings: Pulmonary, hepatic, thyroid, ocular, neurologic, and cardiac toxicities require clinical monitoring.

notes: Amiodarone pharmacokinetics are complex because of tissue accumulation and active metabolites.
Do not infer clinical response or dose adjustment from Tmax alone.

Clinical effects and toxicities may persist after discontinuation.

substances.name: Adenosine
toxicities: Adenosine can cause transient AV block, bradycardia, flushing, chest discomfort, dyspnea, bronchospasm, hypotension, and rare serious arrhythmias.
Effects are very short-lived because adenosine is rapidly cleared from blood.
substances.summary: 腺苷是超短效静脉用 AV 结传导阻滞相关药物；本条目用于时间进程和相互作用资料索引。 Adenosine is an ultra-short-acting IV AV nodal blocker used in selected supraventricular tachycardia contexts. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia contexts depending on label
contraindications: Second- or third-degree AV block without pacemaker
Sick sinus syndrome without pacemaker
Known or suspected bronchoconstrictive or bronchospastic lung disease in some labels
Hypersensitivity
majorWarnings: Can cause transient or prolonged AV block, bradycardia, and arrhythmias.
Bronchospasm can occur in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Dipyridamole can potentiate adenosine effect.
Methylxanthines such as caffeine or theophylline can antagonize adenosine effect.
Carbamazepine may increase heart block risk.
monitoring: Readiness for resuscitation in monitored clinical setting

onset.note: Very rapid onset after IV bolus; exact timing is administration-dependent.

durationOfAction.note: Effects are generally very brief because adenosine is rapidly taken up and metabolized.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Often described as less than 10 seconds.

notes: Adenosine timing is highly route/administration dependent and should not be generalized to oral supplements.
Continuous ECG and clinical monitoring are central.

tdm.monitoringType: not monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Adenosine is ultra-short acting and monitored by immediate ECG/clinical response rather than plasma concentration.

dangerous: Second- or third-degree AV block without pacemaker

toxicities: Excessive nifedipine effect or overdose can cause hypotension, reflex tachycardia or bradycardia, shock, and metabolic disturbances.
Immediate-release and extended-release products have different time-course profiles and should not be treated as interchangeable timeline entries.
substances.summary: 硝苯地平缓释/控释制剂的独立临床药理索引；不得套用即释胶囊的时间进程。

drugClass: 二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂
indications: Hypertension
Angina depending on formulation and local label
majorWarnings: Can cause symptomatic hypotension, edema, flushing, headache, and reflex tachycardia.
Immediate-release formulations have safety concerns in some cardiovascular contexts; follow local approved labeling.
Immediate-release and extended-release nifedipine must not be treated as interchangeable time courses.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A inhibitors can increase nifedipine exposure.
Other antihypertensives can increase blood pressure-lowering effect.
CYP3A inhibitors or inducers may alter exposure; verify the formulation-specific label.

notes: Labels describe a second small peak or shoulder around 6-12 hours for some extended-release tablets.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical use is usually guided by blood pressure, heart rate, symptoms, edema, and interacting drugs rather than plasma concentration.

substances.name: Clopidogrel
toxicities: Clopidogrel can cause bleeding, including serious and fatal bleeding.
Effect depends on metabolic activation; CYP2C19 poor metabolizer status can reduce active metabolite formation.
substances.summary: 氯吡格雷是不可逆 P2Y12 抑制剂抗血小板前药；本条目不作为抗血小板治疗建议。 Clopidogrel is an irreversible P2Y12 inhibitor antiplatelet prodrug. This entry is educational reference only and is not antiplatelet management guidance.

indications: Reduction of atherothrombotic events in selected cardiovascular contexts depending on label
contraindications: Active pathological bleeding
Hypersensitivity to clopidogrel or product components
majorWarnings: Bleeding risk increases with other antithrombotic drugs and bleeding-prone conditions.
Reduced CYP2C19 function can reduce active metabolite exposure and platelet inhibition.
majorInteractions: NSAIDs, warfarin, SSRIs, SNRIs, and other antiplatelet agents can increase bleeding risk.
Opioids can delay and reduce clopidogrel exposure.
CYP2C19-related interactions can alter active metabolite formation.
monitoring: Genotype or platelet function testing only in selected contexts
Platelet function testing only in selected contexts

durationOfAction.note: Platelet inhibition is irreversible for exposed platelets; recovery depends on platelet turnover.

washout.note: Labels commonly describe platelet aggregation and bleeding time returning toward baseline after about 5 days.

notes: Clopidogrel is a prodrug; active metabolite exposure is not the same as duration of platelet effect.
Do not use this field to plan surgery or antiplatelet interruption.
Platelet inhibition and recovery can outlast parent-drug plasma exposure.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clopidogrel response is generally followed clinically; genotype or platelet function testing may be considered only in selected contexts.
interpretationCaveats: This is antiplatelet effect monitoring, not routine plasma concentration TDM.

doseUseReferences.population: Adults or source-specific population; verify cited source

substances.name: Apixaban
toxicities: Apixaban can cause serious and fatal bleeding.
Premature discontinuation can increase thrombotic risk in patients without adequate alternative anticoagulation.
substances.summary: 阿哌沙班是直接 Xa 因子抑制剂抗凝药；本条目区分药代时间进程和偶发的抗凝活性检测。 Apixaban is a direct factor Xa inhibitor anticoagulant. This entry separates PK time course from occasional anticoagulant activity testing.

indications: Stroke/systemic embolism risk reduction and venous thromboembolism contexts depending on label
contraindications: Active pathological bleeding
Severe hypersensitivity to apixaban
majorWarnings: Bleeding risk increases with other anticoagulants, antiplatelets, thrombolytics, SSRIs/SNRIs, and NSAIDs.
Routine clotting tests are not reliable measures of apixaban exposure at therapeutic dosing.
majorInteractions: Combined strong CYP3A4 and P-gp inhibitors increase apixaban exposure.
Combined strong CYP3A4 and P-gp inducers decrease apixaban exposure and may increase thromboembolic risk.
monitoring: Anti-Xa calibrated assays only in selected contexts

notes: PT/INR/aPTT changes are small and variable at expected therapeutic dosing.
Anti-Xa testing, when used, is anticoagulant activity/exposure assessment rather than routine TDM.
Anticoagulant effect and laboratory interpretation are assay- and context-dependent.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration; anti-Xa activity may be used in selected scenarios
tdm.reason: Routine anticoagulation management usually does not use plasma concentration targets for apixaban.
interpretationCaveats: This is not routine TDM and depends heavily on assay calibration, timing, indication, renal function, bleeding/thrombotic context, and local policy.
Routine plasma concentration monitoring is not standard.
Drug-specific calibrated anti-Xa or dilute thrombin/ecarin-based assays are reserved for selected clinical scenarios.

unsafe: Strong combined CYP3A4 and P-gp inhibitors
Strong combined CYP3A4 and P-gp inducers

substances.name: Warfarin
substances.summary: 华法林是维生素 K 拮抗剂抗凝药；常规临床监测重点是 PT/INR 等抗凝效应，而不是血浆华法林浓度。 Warfarin is a vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant. Routine clinical monitoring is based on anticoagulant effect such as PT/INR rather than plasma warfarin concentration.

indications: Thromboembolism prophylaxis and treatment
majorWarnings: Bleeding risk is linked to anticoagulant effect, patient factors, interacting drugs, and monitoring.
majorInteractions: Many drugs, foods, and illness states can alter INR response.

tdm.monitoringType: PT/INR anticoagulant effect monitoring rather than plasma drug concentration
tdm.reason: Warfarin is routinely adjusted and interpreted by INR response, not by plasma warfarin concentration.
tdm.samplingTime: Follow anticoagulation clinic protocol and local approved labeling.

therapeuticRanges.note: DailyMed labels describe this INR range for many indications; some indications require different targets.

interpretationCaveats: This is not plasma concentration TDM.
INR interpretation depends on indication, bleeding/thrombotic risk, timing, interacting drugs, diet, illness, adherence, and local protocol.
Warfarin is adjusted by INR rather than routine plasma drug concentration.

notes: Warfarin clinical monitoring is INR, not routine plasma concentration. Anticoagulant effect reflects vitamin K-dependent clotting factor turnover.
Anticoagulant onset and offset depend on clotting-factor turnover, not plasma warfarin concentration alone.

substances.name: Metoprolol
toxicities: Beta blocker toxicity can include bradycardia, hypotension, AV block, cardiogenic shock, bronchospasm, hypoglycemia masking, seizures, and cardiac arrest in severe overdose.
Clinical management of overdose is based on symptoms, ECG, hemodynamics, glucose/electrolytes, and supportive toxicology care rather than routine plasma concentration.
substances.summary: 美托洛尔是 β1 选择性阻滞剂；本条目区分普通片和缓释制剂的时间进程概念，血药浓度通常不是常规监测目标。 Metoprolol is a beta-1 selective blocker. This entry separates immediate-release and extended-release time-course concepts and marks plasma concentration monitoring as not routine.

indications: Hypertension
Angina
Selected heart failure and arrhythmia contexts depending on formulation and label
majorWarnings: Abrupt withdrawal can worsen angina or precipitate cardiac events in susceptible patients.
Bradycardia, hypotension, heart block, bronchospasm, and masking of hypoglycemia symptoms are clinically relevant safety concerns.
majorInteractions: CYP2D6 inhibitors can increase metoprolol exposure.
Other agents that slow heart rate or AV conduction may increase bradycardia or conduction risk.

onset.note: DailyMed describes significant beta-blocking effect within 1 hour after oral administration.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Poor CYP2D6 metabolizers may have a longer half-life.

notes: Effect duration is dose-related and formulation-specific.
Plasma concentration is not the usual clinical monitoring endpoint.

durationOfAction.note: Extended-release products are designed for once-daily beta-blockade; do not infer dose guidance from this field.

notes: Immediate-release and extended-release formulations should not be collapsed into one time-course record.

CYP2D6 phenotype and inhibitors can alter exposure.

dangerous: Severe bradycardia or heart block without pacing

substances.name: Propranolol
toxicities: Nonselective beta blocker toxicity can include severe bradycardia, hypotension, bronchospasm, heart failure, seizures, and cardiac arrest.
Propranolol overdose can be clinically severe because of beta blockade and membrane-stabilizing effects at high exposure.

indications: Hypertension

contraindications: Cardiogenic shock

majorWarnings: Abrupt discontinuation may exacerbate angina or precipitate myocardial infarction in susceptible patients.
Can worsen bronchospasm and heart failure in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Quinidine, amiodarone, propafenone, verapamil, diltiazem, digoxin, and lidocaine interactions may be clinically important.
CYP2D6, CYP1A2, or CYP2C19 pathway interactions can alter exposure or effect.

substances.name: Atenolol
toxicities: Beta blocker overdose can cause lethargy, bradycardia, hypotension, bronchospasm, heart failure, hypoglycemia, and conduction disturbances.
Atenolol is renally eliminated and can be removed by hemodialysis in overdose according to labeling.
substances.summary: 阿替洛尔是 β1 选择性阻滞剂，并具有 II 类抗心律失常相关性；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Atenolol is a beta-1 selective blocker with Class II antiarrhythmic relevance. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Hypertension
Angina
Selected post-myocardial infarction contexts depending on label
contraindications: Sinus bradycardia
Heart block greater than first degree
Cardiogenic shock
Overt cardiac failure
majorWarnings: Beta blockade can precipitate or worsen cardiac failure in susceptible patients.
Abrupt discontinuation may worsen ischemic heart disease.
majorInteractions: Calcium channel blockers, disopyramide, amiodarone, catecholamine-depleting drugs, and clonidine withdrawal can be clinically important.

notes: Atenolol undergoes little or no hepatic metabolism and is primarily renally eliminated.
Routine clinical monitoring is not plasma concentration based.

tdm.reason: Clinical use is monitored by heart rate, blood pressure, renal function, symptoms, and ECG context rather than plasma concentration.

substances.name: Carvedilol
toxicities: Carvedilol overdose may cause severe hypotension, bradycardia, cardiac insufficiency, cardiogenic shock, cardiac arrest, bronchospasm, vomiting, loss of consciousness, and seizures.
Alpha-1 blockade contributes to orthostatic hypotension risk.
substances.summary: 卡维地洛是非选择性 β 受体阻滞剂，并具有 α1 阻滞作用；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Carvedilol is a nonselective beta blocker with alpha-1 blocking activity. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Heart failure
Hypertension
Left ventricular dysfunction after myocardial infarction depending on label
contraindications: Bronchial asthma or related bronchospastic conditions
Second- or third-degree AV block
Sick sinus syndrome
Severe bradycardia without pacemaker
Cardiogenic shock or decompensated heart failure requiring IV inotropes
Severe hepatic impairment
majorWarnings: Can cause hypotension, bradycardia, worsening heart failure, bronchospasm, and masking of hypoglycemia.
majorInteractions: CYP2D6 inhibitors may increase carvedilol levels.
Digoxin, amiodarone, verapamil, diltiazem, clonidine, and hypotensive agents may be clinically important.

notes: Food slows absorption and can reduce orthostatic hypotension risk according to labeling.

substances.name: Esmolol
toxicities: Esmolol overdose can cause severe bradycardia, AV block, hypotension, cardiac failure, cardiogenic shock, cardiac arrest, bronchospasm, hyperkalemia, hypoglycemia, seizures, and coma.
Its short half-life makes continuous clinical and hemodynamic monitoring central to interpretation.
substances.summary: 艾司洛尔是短效静脉用 β1 选择性阻滞剂；本条目用于快速起效和消退相关资料索引。 Esmolol is a short-acting IV beta-1 selective blocker used when rapid titration of beta blockade is needed. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Supraventricular tachycardia and rapid ventricular rate contexts depending on label
Perioperative or urgent beta blockade contexts depending on label
contraindications: Severe sinus bradycardia
Heart block greater than first degree
Sick sinus syndrome
Decompensated heart failure
Cardiogenic shock
Close-proximity IV cardiodepressant calcium-channel antagonist use such as verapamil
majorWarnings: Can cause severe hypotension, bradycardia, heart block, cardiac failure, and cardiac arrest.
Requires continuous monitoring in clinical use.
majorInteractions: Other drugs lowering blood pressure, myocardial contractility, sinus node function, or AV conduction can exaggerate toxicity.
Digitalis glycosides, verapamil, diltiazem, and other beta blockers are clinically important examples.
monitoring: Signs of heart failure or shock

onset.note: Steady-state blood levels are obtained within about 5 minutes with an appropriate loading dose.

timeToSteadyState.note: About 30 minutes without a loading dose.

notes: Esmolol is rapidly hydrolyzed by red blood cell esterases.
Short plasma half-life does not remove the need for continuous hemodynamic monitoring.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Esmolol is titrated and monitored by continuous clinical/hemodynamic response rather than plasma concentration.

unsafe: Drugs that reduce myocardial contractility or AV conduction

substances.name: Diltiazem
toxicities: Diltiazem can cause bradycardia, AV block, hypotension, worsening heart failure, and clinically significant overdose toxicity.
Overdose assessment is clinical; blood levels vary widely and are not a routine management target.
substances.summary: 地尔硫卓是非二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂；本条目强调剂型相关时间进程、传导毒性和相互作用索引。 Diltiazem is a non-dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker. This entry emphasizes formulation-specific time course, conduction toxicity, and interaction indexing.

indications: Hypertension
Angina
Selected rate-control contexts depending on formulation and label
contraindications: Sick sinus syndrome without a functioning pacemaker
Second- or third-degree AV block without a functioning pacemaker
Hypotension
Acute myocardial infarction with pulmonary congestion in some labels
majorWarnings: Can prolong AV nodal conduction and cause bradycardia or AV block.
May worsen heart failure in susceptible patients.
Bradycardia, AV block, and worsening heart failure can occur in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Beta blockers and digoxin may have additive effects on cardiac conduction.
Diltiazem is a CYP3A4 substrate and inhibitor; CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers can affect exposure and interacting substrates.
Some statins can have increased exposure and myopathy risk with diltiazem.
CYP3A inhibitors or inducers may alter exposure; verify the formulation-specific label.
monitoring: ECG or conduction symptoms when clinically indicated

notes: Diltiazem absorption and Tmax are formulation-specific; immediate-release and extended-release records should not be collapsed.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Routine monitoring is based on heart rate, blood pressure, ECG/conduction symptoms, heart failure symptoms, and interacting drugs rather than plasma concentration.
interpretationCaveats: DailyMed overdose discussion notes wide variability in blood levels, limiting usefulness in overdose cases.

substances.name: Cilostazol
toxicities: Cilostazol is contraindicated in heart failure of any severity because other PDE3 inhibitors have decreased survival in heart failure populations.
Overdose may cause severe headache, diarrhea, hypotension, tachycardia, and possibly cardiac arrhythmias.
substances.summary: 西洛他唑是 PDE3 抑制剂，具有抗血小板和血管扩张作用；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Cilostazol is a PDE3 inhibitor with antiplatelet and vasodilator effects used for intermittent claudication. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Intermittent claudication
contraindications: Heart failure of any severity
Hypersensitivity to cilostazol or product components
majorWarnings: Contraindicated in heart failure of any severity.
Can cause tachycardia, palpitations, hypotension, and other cardiovascular effects.
Cilostazol is contraindicated in heart failure of any severity in the cited US labeling.
majorInteractions: Strong or moderate CYP3A4 inhibitors can increase cilostazol exposure.
CYP2C19 inhibitors can increase exposure to active metabolites.
Diltiazem, erythromycin, ketoconazole, grapefruit juice, and omeprazole are examples described in labels.
monitoring: Platelet function testing only in selected contexts

eliminationHalfLife.note: Apparent elimination half-life for cilostazol and active metabolites.

notes: Symptomatic walking improvement is a repeated-dose clinical outcome and is not equal to plasma Tmax.
The cited source does not encode a single universal value; add exact label-verified Tmax from the selected label before displaying numeric Tmax.
Platelet inhibition and recovery can outlast parent-drug plasma exposure.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical use is usually guided by symptoms, contraindications, bleeding/cardiovascular safety, and interacting drugs rather than plasma concentration.
interpretationCaveats: CYP3A4 and CYP2C19 inhibitors can increase exposure, but this is handled through labeling and clinical monitoring rather than routine TDM.

doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Twice daily before or after meals as specified by the cited label

substances.name: Digoxin
substances.summary: 地高辛是强心苷类药物，具有较窄治疗窗；它适合展示 time course 与真正 TDM 资料的分离。 Digoxin is a cardiac glycoside with a narrow therapeutic index. It is a priority example for separating time-course data from true therapeutic drug monitoring.

indications: Heart failure
Atrial fibrillation rate control in selected contexts
majorWarnings: Digoxin has a narrow therapeutic index and toxicity can occur at lower concentrations in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Renal function changes, electrolyte abnormalities, and interacting drugs can markedly affect toxicity risk.
monitoring: Serum digoxin concentration when clinically indicated

tdm.monitoringType: serum concentration
tdm.samplingTime: At least 6 hours after the last dose; trough at steady state is commonly preferred when clinically feasible.

therapeuticRanges.note: Labcorp cites this lower heart-failure range; local policy may differ.

toxicityThresholds.note: DailyMed notes increased toxicity risk above this concentration, without increased benefit.

interpretationCaveats: Interpret with sampling time, renal function, potassium, magnesium, ECG, symptoms, age, and interacting drugs.
Serum concentration alone does not rule in or rule out toxicity.
Do not mix serum concentration ranges with timeCourse duration or Tmax fields.

notes: Serum digoxin concentrations should generally be interpreted at least 6 hours after the last dose and with potassium, renal function, ECG, symptoms, and interacting drugs.
Intravenous effect timing and serum concentration interpretation still require post-distribution timing and clinical context.

doseUseReferences.indication: Maintenance therapy in the cited oral tablet label
doseUseReferences.population: Adults; indication- and label-specific
doseUseReferences.amountText: 0.0625-0.25 mg once daily in commonly cited adult maintenance contexts
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Once daily; individualized by renal function, age, lean body weight, response, and concentration timing

ranges.label: Maintenance reference
ranges.note: This is a label- or literature-reported regimen reference, not a dosing recommendation. It must not be used for self-medication or dose adjustment.

substances.name: Amlodipine
substances.summary: 氨氯地平是二氢吡啶类钙通道阻滞剂，用于心血管药理学习资料索引；本条目不构成处方或剂量调整建议。 Amlodipine is a dihydropyridine calcium channel blocker used in hypertension and angina. This entry is for educational reference and data indexing only, not prescribing guidance.

indications: Hypertension
Chronic stable angina
Vasospastic angina
majorWarnings: Symptomatic hypotension may occur in susceptible patients.
Worsening angina or acute myocardial infarction has been reported after starting or increasing calcium channel blocker therapy, particularly in severe obstructive coronary artery disease.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A inhibitors may increase amlodipine exposure.
Other antihypertensives may increase blood pressure-lowering effect.
CYP3A inhibitors or inducers may alter exposure; verify the formulation-specific label.

durationOfAction.note: DailyMed states antihypertensive effectiveness is maintained for at least 24 hours with chronic once-daily oral administration.

notes: Tmax and peak clinical effect may not be identical.
Values vary by formulation, population, hepatic function, renal function, and interacting drugs.
This entry does not encode clinical dose guidance.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Amlodipine therapy is usually followed by clinical response and safety findings such as blood pressure, edema, and hypotension symptoms rather than plasma drug concentration.
interpretationCaveats: PK parameters such as Tmax and half-life belong in timeCourse, not TDM ranges.
This record does not provide dose adjustment guidance.

substances.name: Nitroglycerin
substances.summary: 硝酸甘油是有机硝酸酯类血管扩张药；不同给药途径和剂型的起效与持续时间差异很大，本条目仅用于学习索引。 Nitroglycerin, also known as glyceryl trinitrate, is an organic nitrate vasodilator used for angina-related indications. This entry is for educational reference and data indexing only, not prescribing guidance.

indications: Angina pectoris
contraindications: Use with PDE-5 inhibitors or soluble guanylate cyclase stimulators is contraindicated in many nitroglycerin labels because severe hypotension may occur.
majorWarnings: Severe hypotension, particularly with upright posture, may occur.
Tolerance can develop with continuous nitrate exposure; labels commonly include a nitrate-free interval for transdermal systems.
PDE5 inhibitors and soluble guanylate cyclase stimulators can cause severe hypotension.
Continuous exposure can produce nitrate tolerance.
majorInteractions: PDE-5 inhibitors and soluble guanylate cyclase stimulators may cause severe hypotension.
Other vasodilators, alcohol, or antihypertensives may increase hypotensive effects.
monitoring: Nitrate tolerance and nitrate-free interval where formulation-appropriate

peakEffect.note: Digital plethysmography data in the label reports maximum vasodilatory effect by 5 minutes postdose.

durationOfAction.note: DailyMed states effects persist for at least 25 minutes after administration.

notes: Sublingual nitroglycerin time course reflects pharmacodynamic vasodilatory effect, not a TDM target.
Tmax is not listed here because this label passage describes effect timing rather than plasma concentration peak.
Onset and duration are route- and formulation-specific.

durationOfAction.note: DailyMed describes a daily patch-on period of 12 to 14 hours.

eliminationHalfLife.note: After patch removal, plasma concentration declines with a half-life of about an hour.

timeToSteadyState.note: Steady-state plasma concentrations are reached by about 2 hours after patch application.

washout.note: DailyMed describes a daily patch-off period of 10 to 12 hours to reduce tolerance.

notes: Patch timing is formulation-specific and should not be transferred to sublingual or IV nitroglycerin.
This entry does not encode clinical dose guidance.
Onset and duration are route- and formulation-specific.
The cited source does not encode a single universal value; add label-verified onset, effect titration timing, and offset for the specific IV formulation before displaying numeric values.
IV nitroglycerin is included here to validate multiple route/formulation records without inferring values from sublingual or transdermal data.
Onset and duration are route- and formulation-specific.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Nitroglycerin clinical use is guided by symptoms, blood pressure, formulation-specific response, and safety monitoring rather than routine plasma concentration.
interpretationCaveats: Sublingual, transdermal, and IV products have different time-course behavior.
TDM ranges are not encoded for nitroglycerin in this data pass.
