substances.name: Spironolactone
toxicities: Spironolactone can cause hyperkalemia, especially with renal impairment or potassium-raising drugs.
Overdose may cause drowsiness, confusion, rash, nausea, vomiting, dizziness, diarrhea, hyponatremia, or hyperkalemia.
substances.summary: 螺内酯是盐皮质激素受体拮抗剂和保钾利尿剂；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Spironolactone is a mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist and potassium-sparing diuretic. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Heart failure and edema contexts depending on label
Hypertension
Primary hyperaldosteronism
Androgen blockade in feminizing hormone therapy under specialist guideline context
contraindications: Hyperkalemia
Addison disease
Concomitant eplerenone use
majorWarnings: Hyperkalemia can be fatal and requires potassium and renal function monitoring.
Hyperkalemia risk is increased by renal impairment and other potassium-raising therapies.
majorInteractions: ACE inhibitors, ARBs, aliskiren, potassium supplements, and other potassium-sparing drugs can increase hyperkalemia risk.
NSAIDs may reduce diuretic/antihypertensive effect and affect renal function.
Lithium toxicity risk may increase.
monitoring: Total testosterone and estradiol when used in feminizing hormone therapy

tmax.note: Mean Tmax is about 2.6 h for spironolactone and 4.3 h for canrenone.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Parent spironolactone half-life; active metabolites persist longer.

notes: Clinical potassium and diuretic effects depend on active metabolites and physiology, not parent Tmax alone.
Do not use this record for dose adjustment.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Monitoring focuses on potassium, renal function, blood pressure, and volume status rather than plasma spironolactone concentration.
interpretationCaveats: Hyperkalemia risk is a safety monitoring issue, not a plasma concentration TDM range.

mechanisms: Variable suppression of testosterone synthesis in feminizing hormone therapy
affectedHormones: Estradiol indirectly through the combined regimen
assayCaveats: Plasma spironolactone concentration is not used to measure androgen blockade.
Testosterone suppression is variable and must be interpreted with the complete hormone regimen.

substances.name: Atorvastatin
toxicities: Statins can cause myopathy, rhabdomyolysis, liver enzyme abnormalities, and rare immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy.
Risk is increased by interacting drugs, higher exposure, renal impairment, older age, and other patient factors.
substances.summary: 阿托伐他汀是 HMG-CoA 还原酶抑制剂，用于降脂和心血管风险相关资料索引；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Atorvastatin is an HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor used for lipid lowering and cardiovascular risk reduction. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Hyperlipidemia
Cardiovascular risk reduction depending on label and guideline context
contraindications: Active liver disease or unexplained persistent transaminase elevations
Pregnancy
majorWarnings: Myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk increases with higher exposure and interacting drugs.
Liver enzyme abnormalities can occur.
Myopathy and rhabdomyolysis risk increases with interacting drugs or higher exposure.
Pregnancy labeling and reproductive context must be checked before use.
majorInteractions: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors can increase atorvastatin exposure.
Cyclosporine, gemfibrozil, some antivirals, macrolides, azoles, and grapefruit juice can increase myopathy risk.
monitoring: Creatine kinase when muscle toxicity is suspected

eliminationHalfLife.note: Plasma elimination half-life is about 14 hours.

durationOfAction.note: Half-life of inhibitory activity for HMG-CoA reductase is longer because active metabolites contribute.

notes: LDL-C response evolves over repeated dosing and is not described by a single Tmax.
Routine clinical follow-up uses lipid response and safety monitoring, not plasma atorvastatin concentration.
LDL-C response develops over repeated treatment and is distinct from plasma Tmax or elimination half-life.

substances.name: Bicalutamide
substances.summary: 比卡鲁胺是非甾体雄激素受体抑制剂。它阻断雄激素作用，但单药使用时不会可靠降低血清睾酮；女性化 HRT 语境的证据有限，并存在严重肝损伤风险。

indications: Metastatic prostate cancer in combination with an LHRH analogue under the cited label
Feminizing HRT has limited evidence and is not an approved indication
contraindications: Product-label contraindications apply
Pregnancy
majorWarnings: Severe liver injury, including hepatic failure, has been reported.
Serum testosterone may remain high or rise because androgen production is not directly suppressed.
majorInteractions: CYP3A4 substrate interactions may alter exposure.
Coumarin anticoagulant effects and prothrombin time can increase.
monitoring: Total testosterone and estradiol for context only

sourceRefs.title: UCSF Overview of feminizing hormone therapy

mechanisms: Competitive inhibition of androgen receptor signaling
affectedHormones: Total testosterone may remain unchanged or increase
assayCaveats: Serum testosterone does not measure androgen-receptor blockade and is not useful for titrating bicalutamide exposure.

tmax.note: Mean 31.3 hours with SD 14.6 hours for the active enantiomer.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Mean 5.8 days with SD 2.29 days for the active enantiomer.

timeToSteadyState.note: Approximate accumulation window; not a clinical-effect endpoint.

notes: Androgen-receptor blockade must not be interpreted as testosterone production suppression.

doseUseReferences.indication: Metastatic prostate cancer in combination with an LHRH analogue under the cited label
doseUseReferences.population: Adults in the approved oncology context
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Once daily with an LHRH analogue in the cited label
doseUseReferences.note: This oncology label regimen is not a feminizing HRT recommendation.

caveats: Blood testosterone does not quantify androgen-receptor blockade.
Do not use this entry as a testosterone-suppression model.

substances.name: Leuprolide Acetate
substances.summary: 醋酸亮丙瑞林是 GnRH 激动剂；持续给药会先产生短暂刺激，随后下调受体并抑制 LH、FSH 和性腺类固醇。本条目区分产品标签的中枢性性早熟用途与青春期抑制/HRT 资料语境。

indications: Central precocious puberty under the cited pediatric label
Puberty suppression requires specialist guideline-based care
contraindications: Pregnancy
Hypersensitivity to GnRH-related products or formulation components
majorWarnings: Initial gonadotropin and sex-steroid flare
Psychiatric symptoms, seizures, and intracranial hypertension are product-label safety concerns.
majorInteractions: No routine plasma concentration TDM is established.

mechanisms: Initial GnRH receptor stimulation followed by receptor downregulation and suppression of LH and FSH.
monitoringLabs: Pubertal stage, growth velocity, and bone age
assayCaveats: Hormone results depend on depot formulation, time since injection, suppression phase, and assay method.

tmax.note: Product-specific peak times across cited depot formulations.

notes: An initial agonist flare precedes sustained suppression.
Depot strengths and release characteristics are not interchangeable.

doseUseReferences.indication: Central precocious puberty under the cited pediatric label
doseUseReferences.population: Pediatric product-label population
doseUseReferences.amountText: 7.5-45 mg across product-specific LUPRON DEPOT-PED strengths
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Every 1, 3, or 6 months according to the selected formulation

ranges.label: CPP label formulations
ranges.note: Strengths and intervals are not interchangeable.

doseUseReferences.note: This pediatric label index is not an HRT dosing recommendation.

caveats: Initial flare and depot suppression must be modeled separately.
Current data are readiness metadata only.

substances.name: Histrelin Implant
substances.summary: 组氨瑞林植入剂是长效 GnRH 激动剂。持续释放会抑制 LH、FSH 和性腺类固醇；产品标签要求植入满 12 个月后取出或更换。

indications: Central precocious puberty under the cited pediatric label
Puberty suppression requires specialist guideline-based care
contraindications: Pregnancy
Hypersensitivity to GnRH-related products
majorWarnings: Initial hormone flare
Implant localization, removal, breakage, or extrusion problems
Psychiatric symptoms and intracranial hypertension are label concerns.

mechanisms: Initial stimulation followed by GnRH receptor downregulation and sustained gonadotropin suppression.
assayCaveats: Implant age and sample timing must be recorded when interpreting suppression.

notes: Median serum histrelin remained quantifiable through the treatment period.
The implant must be removed after 12 months; a new implant may be placed if treatment continues.

doseUseReferences.indication: Central precocious puberty under the cited pediatric label
doseUseReferences.population: Pediatric product-label population
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Provides drug for 12 months, then must be removed or replaced
doseUseReferences.note: This pediatric label index is not an HRT dosing recommendation.

caveats: Implant insertion and removal dates are required.
Current data are readiness metadata only.

substances.name: Goserelin
substances.summary: 戈舍瑞林是皮下缓释 GnRH 激动剂。其监管标签主要覆盖肿瘤和部分妇科用途；跨性别成人或青春期抑制语境应按专科指南单独解释。

indications: Approved oncology and gynecologic contexts in the cited label
HRT use is context-dependent and not established by this label
contraindications: Pregnancy
Hypersensitivity
majorWarnings: Initial hormone flare
Bone-density loss
Metabolic and cardiovascular risks in androgen-deprivation contexts
majorInteractions: QT-prolonging context may require review.

mechanisms: Initial stimulation followed by GnRH receptor downregulation and suppression of LH and FSH.
monitoringLabs: Bone and metabolic markers when indicated
assayCaveats: Depot strength and time since implantation materially affect interpretation.

tmax.note: Mean 1.8 hours with SD 0.34 hours for the 10.8 mg depot.

notes: Initial agonist flare precedes sustained suppression.
The 3.6 mg and 10.8 mg depot concentration profiles differ.

doseUseReferences.indication: Approved oncology or gynecologic contexts in the cited label
doseUseReferences.population: Product-label population
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Every 28 days or every 12 weeks according to product strength
doseUseReferences.note: This label index is not an HRT dosing recommendation.

caveats: Initial flare, depot-specific release, and the administration interval must be modeled separately.
Current data are readiness metadata only.

sourceRefs.title: UCSF Overview of feminizing hormone therapy

substances.name: Triptorelin
substances.summary: 曲普瑞林是长效 GnRH 激动剂。本条目采用 TRIPTODUR 中枢性性早熟产品标签记录 24 周制剂，并将青春期抑制语境单独标识。

indications: Central precocious puberty under the cited pediatric label
Puberty suppression requires specialist guideline-based care
contraindications: Pregnancy
Hypersensitivity
majorWarnings: Initial hormone flare
Psychiatric symptoms, seizures, intracranial hypertension, and severe skin reactions are label concerns.

mechanisms: Initial stimulation followed by receptor downregulation and suppression of LH and FSH.
assayCaveats: The 24-week depot interval and sample timing must be recorded.

notes: Initial agonist flare precedes sustained suppression.
Renal or hepatic impairment can increase exposure to non-depot intravenous triptorelin; depot interpretation remains product-specific.

doseUseReferences.indication: Central precocious puberty under the cited pediatric label
doseUseReferences.population: Pediatric patients 2 years and older under the cited label
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Once every 24 weeks
doseUseReferences.note: This pediatric label index is not an HRT dosing recommendation.

caveats: Initial flare, sample timing, and the 24-week depot interval must be modeled separately.
Current data are readiness metadata only.

substances.name: Degarelix
substances.summary: 地加瑞克是注射用 GnRH 拮抗剂，可直接降低 LH、FSH 和睾酮而不产生激动剂初始反跳。现有监管标签为前列腺癌用途，并非标准 HRT 条目。

indications: Advanced prostate cancer under the cited label
Routine HRT use is not established by this label
contraindications: Pregnancy
Hypersensitivity
majorWarnings: QT prolongation context
Injection-site reactions
Metabolic and bone effects of prolonged androgen deprivation
majorInteractions: Review other QT-prolonging therapies.

mechanisms: Competitive GnRH receptor antagonism rapidly reduces LH and FSH without an agonist flare.
monitoringLabs: Metabolic and bone markers when indicated
assayCaveats: Suppression markers and clinical context are more informative than plasma degarelix concentration.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Long terminal half-life reflects slow release from the injection-site depot.

notes: Testosterone suppression begins rapidly without an agonist flare.
The long terminal half-life must not be treated as ordinary duration of action.

doseUseReferences.indication: Advanced prostate cancer under the cited label
doseUseReferences.population: Adults in the approved oncology context
doseUseReferences.amountText: 240 mg loading dose followed by 80 mg maintenance doses
doseUseReferences.scheduleText: Loading dose, then every 28 days

ranges.label: Oncology label regimen
ranges.note: Loading and maintenance phases must not be collapsed into one interchangeable range.

doseUseReferences.note: This oncology label regimen is not an HRT dosing recommendation.

caveats: The cited evidence and regimen are oncology-specific.
Injection timing and the 28-day maintenance interval affect interpretation.
Routine feminizing HRT model use is not established.

substances.name: Relugolix
substances.summary: 瑞卢戈利是口服 GnRH 拮抗剂，可快速抑制 LH、FSH 和睾酮且无激动剂初始反跳。现有监管标签为前列腺癌用途，并非标准 HRT 条目。

indications: Advanced prostate cancer under the cited label
Routine HRT use is not established by this label
contraindications: Pregnancy
Hypersensitivity
majorWarnings: QT prolongation context
Embryo-fetal toxicity
Bone and metabolic effects of prolonged androgen deprivation
majorInteractions: P-gp inhibitors and combined P-gp/strong CYP3A inducers can substantially alter exposure.

mechanisms: Competitive GnRH receptor antagonism rapidly reduces LH and FSH without an agonist flare.
monitoringLabs: Bone and metabolic markers when indicated
assayCaveats: Adherence and time since the last oral dose affect interpretation.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Effective half-life is about 25 hours; mean terminal half-life is about 61 hours.

notes: Testosterone suppression is a pharmacodynamic endpoint distinct from plasma relugolix concentration.
Adherence is important because this is a daily oral antagonist.

doseUseReferences.note: This oncology label regimen is not an HRT dosing recommendation.

caveats: The cited evidence and regimen are oncology-specific.
Routine feminizing HRT model use is not established.

substances.name: Disulfiram
substances.summary: Disulfiram is indexed as a clinical psychiatry medicine. This entry is for clinical reference indexing and education; product-specific labeling should be checked before adding exact pharmacokinetic or regimen values.

indications: Alcohol, opioid, nicotine, or other substance-use-disorder contexts depending on local labeling
contraindications: See current product-specific label and local approved labeling.
majorWarnings: Indication, withdrawal state, opioid exposure, hepatic function, and behavioral supports change interpretation.
majorInteractions: Drug-specific CYP, QT, serotonergic, CNS depressant, or other interaction risks require product-specific label review.
